content: We can reframe a curiosity-driven autonomous agent’s internal intrinsic reward generation process as a dynamically evolving Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) Reward Machine, where each curiosity-driven exploration step updates the reward machine’s state transitions to encode the agent’s growing knowledge of unknown environment patterns. This structure lets curiosity-driven agents systematically prioritize exploratory actions that align with uncompleted long-term exploration goals encoded in the LTL reward structure, rather than just rewarding random novelty.
